category_title: Reserved students who only share knowledge when directly prompted by an adult
minimal_context: The student is participating in a group workshop session to observe and discuss crabs.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's observe these crabs together and share your ideas and questions with the group.

STUDENT: (Watches the crabs quietly while Deisha, Sierra, Abria, and Brandon do most of the talking.)

TUTOR: What else do you all notice?

STUDENT: (Looks at the crabs but remains reserved, not blurting out any ideas.)

TUTOR: Keep discussing what you see with each other.

STUDENT: (Stays quiet despite having good questions, waiting until she has the direct attention of an adult before speaking.)

options.A: Work collaboratively with the student to set goals for engagement during the group workshop sessions.
options.B: Extend the duration of the workshop sessions, allowing thirty minutes rather than fifteen for the activity.
options.C: Record the observation data in scatter plots and ABC charts after the workshop session finishes.
options.D: Encourage compassionate attitudes toward the crabs by discussing their needs, emotions, and similarities to humans.
